title: gastrolobium minus
intro: Gastrolobium minus, also known as the poison or spider flower, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its vibrant red or yellow flowers and toxic properties. The plant contains toxic compounds such as glycosides and alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Gastrolobium minus has been reported to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against cell damage, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution is advised due to its toxic properties and potential side effects.
